demographic_audit_note: Audited Atlantis demographics against facility face sheet; corrected error in patient ZIP code and added missing secondary phone number for the daughter (POA). Verified Medicare Advantage Plan coverage for home health.
home_vs_ltc_determination: Social Worker confirmed the goal is Home with skilled nursing for diabetic wound care and PT for gait training; patient's daughter is available to assist with ADLs.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2: SW reports wound is showing granulation; PT notes patient is now 'Min Assist' for transfers. Target discharge date set for Wednesday the 15th.
v_card_and_flyer_status: V-Card successfully inserted into patient's smartphone and physical program flyer delivered to the bedside during the Week 2 education session.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Pre-DC_Call_Disconnect: Attempted 24-hour pulse call to daughter (POA) three times; all calls went to voicemail. Home readiness and first-visit window remain unconfirmed.
atlantis_final_sync: Entered D/C date of 11/15 and 1st visit date of 11/16 into Atlantis based on the Social Worker's verbal discharge plan.
ma_visit_booking: Medical Assistant (MA) scheduled for 11/16 at 09:00 AM; visit status marked as 'Pending Confirmation' due to failed pulse call reach.
narrative_summary: A 69yo female with a diabetic wound was triaged in Atlantis for a home transition. The PN completed Stage 1 audits and Stage 2 education, ensuring the V-Card was installed. However, a Stage 3 Pre-DC pulse call disconnect prevented verification of the MA visit window, resulting in a transition breakdown when the patient was not home for the scheduled start-of-care.

format_1_state_log.event_description: Weekly alignment with SW regarding wound healing progress.
Mandatory 24-hour pre-discharge pulse call failed; POA unreachable.

format_2_triples.situation: Atlantis demographics missing daughter's secondary contact number.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Audited facility face sheet and updated Atlantis record to ensure reachability.

format_2_triples.situation: Patient's daughter expresses anxiety about managing diabetic wound dressings at home.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Conducted bedside education walking the family through Day 1 expectations and MA support.

format_2_triples.situation: PN cannot reach family 24 hours before discharge to confirm the MA schedule.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Escalated the communication gap to the Social Worker to ensure the visit time is relayed during discharge paperwork.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Wait for the Home Health Agency intake coordinator to confirm they have successfully contacted the family before finalizing the MA schedule in Atlantis.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Respects the HHA's responsibility for intake coordination but risks missing the 24-hour incentive window if the HHA is also delayed.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Flag the unreachable POA to the Social Worker and request they verify the 09:00 AM MA visit time with the family during the physical discharge handoff.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Complies with the PN checklist Stage 3 by using the SW as the primary facility channel to bridge the communication gap without overstepping.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Call the patient's insurance provider to request an alternate emergency contact number or to verify if the patient has a secondary residence listed in their file.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Directly violates the SW Boundary and Banned Actions by contacting insurance companies for administrative data that should be managed by the facility staff.
